perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to keep confidential or protect such Proprietary Information as it would its own confidential and proprietary information at the highest level of proprietary material.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to keep confidential or protect such Proprietary Information using reasonable security measures.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the confidentiality standard in Section 3 from 'highest level of proprietary material' to 'reasonable security measures,' a contradiction arises with Section 1 which still implies a higher level of protection. Changing the measure of confidentiality, creates a discrepancy on what it means to keep something confidential and makes it less severe.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Party receiving Proprietary Information (the "Recipient") and its Representatives will protect all Proprietary Information received under this Agreement from any disclosure to third parties or the use of such Proprietary Information beyond that allowed under this Agreement, in each case, in accordance with this Agreement, and will not engage in such disclosure or use without the prior express written permission of the Party disclosing such information (the "Disclosing Party"). However, the Recipient's protection obligations are limited to commercially reasonable efforts.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the 'highest level of proprietary material' protection, while the changed text lowers this standard to 'reasonable security measures'. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text which states, 'Recipient... will protect all Proprietary Information... in accordance with this Agreement,' which now has a lower standard embedded in it. The change creates an inconsistency in the level of protection required, creating a contradiction that makes enforcement unclear. Even though the contradicted text does state 'However, the Recipient's protection obligations are limited to commercially reasonable efforts' it does not change the fact that a contradiction has been introduced to the agreement. The change means that section 3 and the contradicted text are now in conflict with each other because they are referencing conflicting levels of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: All such Proprietary Information (whether prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives on its behalf) shall be returned or destroyed, at the election of Disclosing Party or upon request of Disclosing Party provided, however, that all notes, analyses, conditions, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents, to the extent that they contain, reflect or are based upon the Proprietary Information, may be destroyed (rather than delivered); provided further that Board or Board Committee minutes need neither be delivered nor destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: All such Proprietary Information (whether prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives on its behalf) shall be returned or destroyed, at the election of Disclosing Party or upon request of Disclosing Party provided, however, that all notes, analyses, conditions, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents, to the extent that they contain, reflect or are based upon the Proprietary Information, need neither be delivered nor destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'provided further that Board or Board Committee minutes need neither be delivered nor destroyed,' from Section 4, there is a contradiction. The original wording provides a specific exception for Board minutes which will now be in conflict with the phrase that Proprietary Information is to be be returned or destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Proprietary Information, including all applicable intellectual property rights residing in the Proprietary Information (including without limitation, patents, copyrights, industrial designs, trademarks and trade secrets), unless otherwise specified in writing by Disclosing Party, are and shall remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, including any and all reproductions in any form of said Proprietary Information. All such Proprietary Information (whether prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives on its behalf) shall be returned or destroyed, at the election of Disclosing Party or upon request of Disclosing Party provided, however, that all notes, analyses, conditions, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents, to the extent that they contain, reflect or are based upon the Proprietary Information, need neither be delivered nor destroyed. Upon the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall execute a certificate of destruction of such Proprietary Information when destroyed by Recipient, whether intentionally or accidentally, and transmit the same to Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides an exception for Board or Board Committee minutes from the requirement to be returned or destroyed. The changed text removes this exception. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text requires all Proprietary Information to be either returned or destroyed. Since the Board minutes falls under Proprietary Information, the omission of the exception means that it now contradicts the requirement of returning or destroying Board minutes.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient hereby agrees that it will not use the Proprietary Information for any reason or purpose other than to evaluate a possible Business Arrangement with the Disclosing Party and that the use of the Proprietary Information supplied hereunder shall be limited to Recipient's activities in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient hereby agrees that it will not use the Proprietary Information for any competitive purpose. The use of the Proprietary Information supplied hereunder shall be limited to Recipient's activities in connection therewith.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'for any reason or purpose other than to evaluate a possible Business Arrangement' to 'for any competitive purpose' in Section 6, the scope of restriction has changed. This creates a possible in-text contradiction because while the agreement specifies evaluating the possible business agreement with the disclosing party, it now allows it to be used for any other reason that is not for competitive purposes. This conflicts with Sections 1 and 3 that outlines that the Recipient should protect the proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Party receiving Proprietary Information (the "Recipient") and its Representatives will protect all Proprietary Information received under this Agreement from any disclosure to third parties or the use of such Proprietary Information beyond that allowed under this Agreement, in each case, in accordance with this Agreement, and will not engage in such disclosure or use without the prior express written permission of the Party disclosing such information (the "Disclosing Party"). However, the Recipient's protection obligations are limited to commercially reasonable efforts.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'for any reason or purpose other than to evaluate a possible Business Arrangement' to 'for any competitive purpose' significantly broadens the permitted use of the Proprietary Information. While the original text restricted use to evaluating a business arrangement, the changed text allows any use that is not for competitive purposes. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the Recipient will protect the Proprietary Information from any use beyond that allowed under this agreement. The changed text now allows for uses that were not previously allowed, directly conflicting with the restriction in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that until the expiry of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, each Party or its parent and affiliated companies (herein, collectively a "Party"), will not, and each Party will direct its parent and affiliated companies not to, except with the prior written consent of the other Party (i) purchase, acquire, obtain or hold (or offer or agree to purchase, acquire, obtain or hold) beneficial ownership of any of the securities of the other Party; (ii) make, or in any way participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any of the other Party's securities; (iii) enter into, or agree to enter into or seek or propose any merger, consolidation, business combination, tender or exchange offer, sale or purchase of assets or securities, dissolution, liquidation, restructuring, recapitalization or similar transaction involving the other Party; (iv) act, alone or together with any person, to control or influence the management or the Board of Directors or other Party; or (v) advise, encourage, or assist any person in the acquisition of beneficial ownership of any of the other Party's securities or control of the other Party or a significant portion of its significant assets; provided, however, that such restrictions (or the restrictions set forth in the next sentence) shall terminate in the event that a third party publicly commences a tender or exchange offer, or otherwise proposes a merger or business combination transaction, in respect of the other Party and such other Party has (a) exempted such transaction for purposes of Section 203 of the Delaware General Corporation Law, or (b) publicly announced or publicly confirmed that it is engaged in negotiations in connection with such proposed transaction; provided, further, any such termination shall not relieve the parties of any obligations hereunder with respect to the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that until the expiry of eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, each Party or its parent and affiliated companies (herein, collectively a "Party"), will not, and each Party will direct its parent and affiliated companies not to, except with the prior written consent of the other Party (i) purchase, acquire, obtain or hold (or offer or agree to purchase, acquire, obtain or hold) beneficial ownership of any of the securities of the other Party; (ii) make, or in any way participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any of the other Party's securities; (iii) enter into, or agree to enter into or seek or propose any merger, consolidation, business combination, tender or exchange offer, sale or purchase of assets or securities, dissolution, liquidation, restructuring, recapitalization or similar transaction involving the other Party; (iv) act, alone or together with any person, to control or influence the management or the Board of Directors or other Party; or (v) advise, encourage, or assist any person in the acquisition of beneficial ownership of any of the other Party's securities or control of the other Party or a significant portion of its significant assets; provided, however, that such restrictions (or the restrictions set forth in the next sentence) shall terminate in the event that a third party publicly commences a tender or exchange offer, or otherwise proposes a merger or business combination transaction, in respect of the other Party and such other Party has (a) exempted such transaction for purposes of Section 203 of the Delaware General Corporation Law, or (b) publicly announced or publicly confirmed that it is engaged in negotiations in connection with such proposed transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'provided, further, any such termination shall not relieve the parties of any obligations hereunder with respect to the Proprietary Information,' from Section 11, a potential in-text contradiction is created. The agreement states that the restrictions will terminate in the event that a third party commences an offer, or a transaction, in respect of the other Party. Now the Proprietary information has no safe-guards in place if an event like such takes place, conflicting with the general statement that the agreement protects proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Party receiving Proprietary Information (the "Recipient") and its Representatives will protect all Proprietary Information received under this Agreement from any disclosure to third parties or the use of such Proprietary Information beyond that allowed under this Agreement, in each case, in accordance with this Agreement, and will not engage in such disclosure or use without the prior express written permission of the Party disclosing such information (the "Disclosing Party"). However, the Recipient's protection obligations are limited to commercially reasonable efforts.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 11 includes a clause stating that the termination of restrictions due to a third-party offer does not relieve parties of obligations regarding Proprietary Information. The changed text omits this clause. The contradicted text requires the Recipient to protect Proprietary Information. By removing the clause about Proprietary Information in Section 11, the changed text now creates a contradiction because the agreement no longer explicitly safeguards Proprietary Information in the event of a third-party offer, conflicting with the general protection obligations outlined in the contradicted text.
